<path>/Users/savekirk/dev/ai/entire/session-bridge/src/runCommand.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: import * as cp from "child_process";
2: 
3: export enum ProcessExitType {
4:   CLOSE,
5:   EXIT
6: }
7: 
8: export interface CommandInput {
9:   command: string;
10:   args?: string[];
11:   onSuccess: (data: string) => void;
12:   onError: (error: Error) => void;
13:   onExit: (type: ProcessExitType, code: number | null) => void;
14: }
15: 
16: 
17: /**
18:  * Utility to execute commands and return their results through callbacks.
19:  * 
20:  * @param {CommandInput} param0 - The inputs and callbacks
21:  * 
22:  * @returns {cp.ChildProcessWithoutNullStreams} - The spawn child process
23:  */
24: export const runCommand = ({ command, args, onSuccess, onError, onExit }: CommandInput): cp.ChildProcessWithoutNullStreams => {
25:   const child = cp.spawn(command, args);
26: 
27:   child.stdout.on('data', (data) => {
28:     onSuccess(data);
29:   });
30: 
31:   child.stderr.on('data', (error) => {
32:     onError(error);
33:   });
34: 
35:   child.on('close', (code) => {
36:     onExit(ProcessExitType.CLOSE, code);
37:   });
38: 
39:   child.on('exit', (code) => {
40:     onExit(ProcessExitType.EXIT, code);
41:   });
42: 
43:   return child;
44: };
45: 

(End of file - total 45 lines)
</content>

<path>/Users/savekirk/dev/ai/entire/session-bridge/src/EntireBinaryResolver.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: import { runCommand } from "./runCommand";
2: export type EntireBinary = {
3:   command: string
4:   cliVersion?: string;
5:   goVersion?: string;
6:   osArch?: string;
7:   raw: string;
8: }
9: 
10: export type EntireResolveError = {
11:   message: string,
12:   reason: string
13: }
14: 
15: function parseVersion(version: string): EntireBinary {
16:   let parts = version.trim().split("\n");
17:   var eb: EntireBinary = { command: "entire", raw: "string" };
18: 
19:   if (parts.length < 3) {
20:     return eb;
21:   }
22: 
23:   const ev = parts[0].split(" ")[2];
24:   if (typeof ev === "string") {
25:     eb.cliVersion = ev.trim();
26:   }
27: 
28:   const gv = parts[1].split(":")[1];
29:   if (typeof gv === "string") {
30:     eb.goVersion = gv.trim();
31:   }
32: 
33:   const os = parts[2].split(":")[1];
34:   if (typeof os === "string") {
35:     eb.osArch = os.trim();
36:   }
37: 
38:   return eb;
39: }
40: 
41: export async function resolveEntireBinary(): Promise<EntireBinary | EntireResolveError> {
42:   return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
43:     runCommand({
44:       command: "entire", args: ["version"], onExit(type, code) {
45:         reject({ message: "Error detecting entire cli. Try again.", reason: `Command exited with exit type: ${type} and code: ${code}` });
46:       }, onError(error) { reject({ message: "Make sure entire cli is installed and available", reason: error.message }); }, onSuccess(data) {
47:         resolve(parseVersion(data));
48:       },
49:     });
50: 
51:   });
52: }
53: 
54: export function isEntireBinary(obj: any): obj is EntireBinary {
55:   return (typeof obj === 'object' && obj !== null && 'command' in obj && typeof obj.command === "string" && 'raw' in obj && typeof obj.raw === 'string');
56: 
57: }

(End of file - total 57 lines)
</content>

metadata.preview:
# session-bridge README

This is the README for your extension "session-bridge". After writing up a brief description, we recommend including the following sections.

## Features

Describe specific features of your extension including screenshots of your extension in action. Image paths are relative to this README file.

For example if there is an image subfolder under your extension project workspace:

\!\[feature X\]\(images/feature-x.png\)

> Tip: Many popular extensions utilize animations. This is an excellent way to show off your extension! We recommend short, focused animations that are easy to follow.

## Requirements

If you have any requirements or dependencies, add a section describing those and how to install and configure them.

## Extension Settings
